_meta.source: Trauma therapy graduated exposure principles + clinical interview research
_meta.purpose: Controls depth of emotional content in voice interview and game narrative

layers.name: Aspirational
layers.description: Values, hopes, preferences. Minimal emotional risk.
example_questions: What kind of world does your character want to live in?
What quality does your character have that they're most proud of?
What matters most to your character?
What's one thing you'd give your character that would make their journey easier?

layers.name: Relational
layers.description: Who matters to the character. Relationships, connection, loss. Moderate emotional content.
layers.unlock_condition: User engagement level is high + session has established safety
example_questions: Who matters most to your character?
Who has your character lost or not connected with?
What kind of friend is your character?
Who does your character wish they could talk to?
layers.game_content: Companion selection, NPC relationships, trust quests

layers.name: Historical
layers.description: What shaped the character. Past experiences. Higher emotional content.
layers.unlock_condition: Layers 1-2 complete + mirror bubble engagement > 0.3 + therapist visibility milestone
example_questions: What has your character been through?
What shaped who your character is today?
What does your character carry from their past?
Is there something your character has never told anyone?
layers.game_content: Backstory quests, narrative depth, antagonist emergence

layers.name: Core Wounds
layers.description: What the character most fears. What they most need. Core material.
layers.unlock_condition: Layer 3 reviewed by therapist + explicit therapist approval to proceed
example_questions: What does your character most fear?
What does your character most need?
What would your character's life look like if they were free of what holds them back?
What does the deepest part of your character want the world to know?
layers.game_content: Final quest arc, antagonist confrontation, resolution

safety_triggers.response: Stop game, warm acknowledgment, crisis line immediate display, therapist notification
Acknowledge without panic, provide therapist hotline, do not continue game

safety_triggers.pattern: Acute psychotic content in user's voice (not character)
safety_triggers.response: Acknowledge the user (not character), provide grounding, pause game
Follow mandatory reporting protocol; escalate to therapist/crisis line
Slow down, offer support, do not push re-engagement

interview_opening_sequence.principle: First 90 seconds establish whether the user feels safe enough to continue.
steps: Establish warmth, not urgency — slow pace, low-pressure greeting
Offer explicit control — 'There's no right way to do this. You're in charge.'
Signal safety through structure — 'I'll ask a few questions. You can skip any time.'
Start with values, not feelings — 'What kind of world does your character want to live in?'
Invite the character before the person — 'Tell me about the person you want to become in this story.'

pacing_rules.silence_is_skill: Never rush to fill silences. Let the user break silence.
soft_continuer_phrases: We can move forward whenever you're ready.
pacing_rules.between_questions: Ask one, wait, reflect before asking another. Never pile on follow-ups.
